content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 21: The reusable content atom

The free 14 days got the reps moving. The paid track is where the habit has to become professionally useful. Today belongs to creation and editing: one practical workout, one usable output, one more piece of your AI working system.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
---------------
CREATION & DRAFTING | PUSH | 20 min

THE BRIEF:
Turn one piece of thinking into several useful formats without flattening it.

THE SITUATION:
Choose one idea from your week. It might be a lesson, observation, decision, or insight. You will turn it into reusable atoms.

PROMPT SCAFFOLD:
Do not copy this blindly. Adapt it to your work.

"I am working on [real situation]. My goal is [specific outcome]. The audience or user is [person/team/customer/reader]. Here is the source material or context I am allowed to share: [paste safe material]. The constraints are [length, tone, deadline, format, privacy, things to avoid]. Help me with: Turn one piece of thinking into several useful formats without flattening it. Before producing the final output, tell me what assumptions you are making and what information would improve the result."

YOUR REPS:
1. Ask the model to create a short note, a longer explanation, a slide headline, a LinkedIn-style paragraph, and a private reminder from the same idea.

2. Compare what changes across formats: audience, length, evidence, and call to action.

3. Pick the format that creates the most value and improve it manually.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
- The formats are genuinely different, not the same text wearing different hats.
- The strongest version has a clear use case.
- You keep one reusable atom for future work.

TRANSFER:
This transfers to emails, updates, proposals, posts, reports, scripts, briefs, and all the places where a first draft normally arrives late wearing a suspicious hat.

COACHING NOTE:
A good idea should not have to be rediscovered every time. The professional advantage is building a reusable knowledge surface from work you were already doing.

BEHAVIOURAL DESIGN:
End the week by adding one saved atom to your personal AI workbench. This is your implementation intention for today: after you finish the workout, decide exactly where this output will live and when you will use it again.

MINIMUM VIABLE REP:
If today is chaos, do the smallest honest version: spend five minutes creating the input and five minutes reviewing the output. Do not aim for a masterpiece. Aim for a usable artefact and one clear improvement. Keeping the chain alive matters because skill comes from repeated contact with real work, not from admiring the concept from a safe distance.

---------------
Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive. Treat AI outputs as drafts, not authority. Verify factual claims before using them in public, financial, medical, legal, HR, or high-stakes contexts.
The AI Habit: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
review_notes: Generated as part of the 90-Day AI Habit paid founding track.
Continues from the free 14-day starter sequence without duplicating the foundation.
Organised by professional capability themes: creation, research, decisions, creativity, management, communication, productivity, analysis, workflow, judgement, and workplace adoption.
Draws from official prompt guidance and behavioural science principles without copying source programme content.
